page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running on the 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most multi-threaded and general compute workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 58004.62, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 12780.0, both falling within the middle 50% of tested servers. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1086.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.63 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth is robust, delivering 2259.49 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 165.27 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

The t2a-standard-32 is suited for scale-out workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded applications. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 30.04 GB/sec and handles 40.8 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. While it performs adequately on software compilation with a Geekbench clang score of 27038.0, it is less efficient for single-threaded tasks and large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for 70B models drop to a poor 2.31 tokens/sec. The lack of bundled local storage means all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate solid multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 58004.62 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12780.0, though single-core performance is comparatively modest. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves 30.04 GB/sec throughput, and database operations like Redis, which registers 40.8 million operations per second. It represents a resource-dense option for ARM-native development, containerized microservices, and scale-out applications that do not require high single-threaded speed or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 128 GB RAM. It is optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads and web serving without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM64 instance with 32 dedicated cores and 128 GB RAM, designed for general-purpose scale-out workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose instance offering thirty-two dedicated cores and balanced memory for multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong multi-threaded performance for web serving and Redis databases
Suited for ARM-native development and containerized microservices
